skill_patches: drupal-views-dev: CRITICAL callout for empty query() on virtual ViewsField plugins
drupal-batch-queue-cron: CRITICAL callout for SuspendQueueException dual catch blocks
drupal-theming: CRITICAL callout for #theme usage and #attached library attachment
patches_that_worked: drupal-views-dev query() override: v1 FAIL → v2 PASS
drupal-batch-queue-cron SuspendQueueException: v1 FAIL → v2 PASS
patches_that_failed: drupal-theming #attached: still FAIL (library declared but never attached)
drupal-theming #theme: still FAIL (hook declared but #markup used instead)

discriminating_expectations.text: Cron detects overdue task and creates queue items

shared_failures.text: #attached library not wired to render arrays
shared_failures.note: Both variants declare .libraries.yml correctly but never attach via #attached. Haiku builds infrastructure but doesn't wire the last connection.

shared_failures.text: #theme key not used in render arrays
shared_failures.note: Both variants declare hook_theme() and create templates but use #markup in actual output. Same 'declaration-usage gap' pattern.

pre_existing_bugs: Drupal\system\Entity\User namespace bug (Phase 14) — both variants, not addressable via skills

runtime_assertion_value.rt-6_cron_queue: Caught accessCheck() bug in WITHOUT that static analysis missed — entity query in hook_cron lacked ->accessCheck(FALSE). Confirmed WITH v2 full chain via watchdog logs.
runtime_assertion_value.design_note: Queue items processed during same cron run (QueueWorker has cron time budget), so checking queue count post-cron finds 0 items. Must verify via watchdog logs instead.
